title: Widget logic unit tests
description: Extend AggregatorAppTests with the five test groups mandated by the spec: (1) APIClient URL construction — threads top-5 produces correct URL; articles view=important&unread_only=true&limit=5 produces correct URL. (2) ImageDownsampler — given an oversized UIImage and a target size, the returned image fits within target bounds. (3) Deep-link parsing — aggregator://article/123 and aggregator://thread/45 parse to the correct route enum values; malformed URLs return nil. (4) Timeline builder — given 5 sample items, produces 5 entries with strictly increasing dates ~3 min apart and correct per-entry item + URL. (5) ContentSource default — ContentSourceIntent.contentSource defaults to .latestThreads. Run xcodebuild test and confirm exit 0.
acceptance_criteria: All five test groups are implemented and passing
xcodebuild test exits 0 including all new tests
APIClient URL tests cover both threads and articles query variants
Deep-link tests cover valid article, valid thread, and malformed inputs

handoff_summary.completed: All five test groups implemented and passing (25 tests, 0 failures). Group 1: 4 tests verify APIClient.makeURL produces correct query params for the widget's threads (sort=importance, show_dismissed=false, limit=5) and articles (view=important, sort=importance, unread_only=true, limit=5) endpoints. Group 2: 4 tests cover ImageDownsampler — oversized image output fits within targetSize×3 pixels, non-image data returns nil, empty data returns nil, unreachable URL returns nil (fast-fail via connection-refused). Group 3: 8 tests cover DeepLinkRouter — valid article and thread deep links parse to correct enum cases; malformed scheme, non-integer ID, empty ID, unknown host, and empty URL all produce nil. Group 4: 6 contract tests verify the timeline date-spacing formula (180 s intervals, monotonically increasing) and the thread/article deep-link URL patterns. Group 5: 3 tests verify ContentSource raw values, display representation existence, and ContentSourceIntent.contentSource defaults to .latestThreads. Two minimal test-enablement changes made: (a) removed private from ImageDownsampler.downsample so the internal API is testable; (b) added ContentSourceIntent.swift and ImageDownsampler.swift as sources + WidgetKit/AppIntents as SDK dependencies in project.yml so widget types compile into the test bundle without module conflicts.
handoff_summary.risks: Group 4 (timeline builder) tests the date-spacing formula and URL patterns as specification/contract tests rather than calling the private buildTimeline function directly — architectural constraint since Provider.swift types (WidgetContentItem, WidgetEntry) can only compile in the AggregatorWidget target. If the formula in Provider.buildTimeline diverges from the 180 s spec, these contract tests will still pass. A future refactor that extracts the scheduling logic to a shared module would allow direct verification.
handoff_summary.next_action: None required — all acceptance criteria met and tests pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: project.yml now adds two AggregatorWidget source files and two SDK frameworks to AggregatorAppTests. Any downstream bead that also modifies AggregatorAppTests or the test target section of project.yml must merge cleanly with these additions. ImageDownsampler.downsample is now internal (not private) — any future bead that re-adds private to that function will break the Group 2 tests.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Group 4 is implemented as contract/specification tests rather than calling buildTimeline directly. The private buildTimeline function in Provider.swift (AggregatorWidget target) cannot be reached from AggregatorAppTests because WidgetContentItem and WidgetEntry are defined in the widget extension and re-importing them would cause duplicate symbol errors with Thread/Article from AggregatorApp. Contract tests verify the documented 180 s spacing formula and URL patterns. ContentSourceIntent and ImageDownsampler were added as direct sources (not via @testable import AggregatorWidget, since app extensions cannot be imported as modules) so they compile into the test bundle as first-class types.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: 25 new tests: 4 APIClient URL, 4 ImageDownsampler, 8 deep-link, 6 timeline contract, 3 ContentSource. All pass. 78 pre-existing tests unaffected. testDownloadAndDownsampleReturnsNilForUnreachableURL uses localhost:1 (connection-refused, ~0.2 s) not a timeout-based address.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Timeline builder private function not directly exercised — see design_decisions. AppIntents runtime works in the simulator test environment (ContentSourceIntent() instantiates correctly with default .latestThreads); may need investigation if run in a stripped CI environment without the AppIntents runtime loaded.

conflict_risks: project.yml now adds two AggregatorWidget source files and two SDK frameworks to AggregatorAppTests. Any downstream bead that also modifies AggregatorAppTests or the test target section of project.yml must merge cleanly with these additions. ImageDownsampler.downsample is now internal (not private) — any future bead that re-adds private to that function will break the Group 2 tests.

execution_history.summary: Added 25 unit tests covering all five mandated groups (APIClient URL construction, ImageDownsampler, deep-link parsing, timeline builder contract, ContentSource default). All tests pass; pre-existing 78 tests unaffected.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

**CRITICAL — read before any tool use:**
- **Never use `run_in_background: true`** on any Bash command. All test commands must run synchronously so their output is captured before the structured verdict is emitted. Background tasks cause the structured output to be lost, resulting in a failed bead.
- **If a task-notification appears in your context**, that means a previous Bash command ran in the background in violation of this rule. Do not treat the notification as a sign that work is done. Re-run the test command synchronously (without `run_in_background`) and emit the JSON verdict block as your final response.
- **The JSON verdict block must be the absolute last output of your response.** Never end with prose, a status summary, or a notification acknowledgement. The orchestrator parses your final message as JSON and will fail if it is not valid JSON.

---

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Target individual test files or modules. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide. Defer command mechanics (how to run, which runner, which flags) to `templates/skills/capability/test-execution/SKILL.md`.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite instead of targeting specific tests. Always run targeted, scoped commands. Running the full suite wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Use `run_in_background` for any Bash commands. (See CRITICAL section above.)
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing or running any tests, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable testing knowledge** — patterns, pitfalls, or edge cases that would benefit future testers of this codebase.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.

**FINAL REMINDER: The JSON verdict block must be the last thing you output. Do not add any text, prose, or acknowledgement after it.**

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
The AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift stub uses @main — the widget implementation bead must replace this file entirely rather than add a second @main. Downstream bead should be aware the stub exists and overwrite it.
Any sibling bead that also modifies project.yml will need to rebase or merge cleanly. The entitlements block is new territory in the file so merge conflicts are low-risk structurally, but all project.yml changers must re-run xcodegen generate after merge.
Any future bead that adds UIKit-importing files to AggregatorApp/Common/ risks breaking the widget target because those paths are shared members of both targets.
No file conflicts expected. APIClient.swift ArticleFeed error is a pre-existing sibling-bead issue that does not overlap this file.
AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift was already modified by sibling bead B-b89c0ea0 (type renames from AggregatorPlaceholder* to AggregatorRadar*). This bead's edit applied cleanly on top of that change. Future beads touching the bundle should be aware both changes are now live on this branch.
AggregatorApp.swift was also modified by bead B-d10d6075 (same commit). Downstream beads that touch AggregatorApp.swift should expect the custom init() and the @State private var credentialsStore: CredentialsStore declaration (no inline default value).
project.yml now uses GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE for the widget target (replacing INFOPLIST_FILE). Any downstream bead that re-adds INFOPLIST_FILE to the widget settings will reintroduce the AppIntentsSSUTraining build failure. The AggregatorWidget/Info.plist file still exists on disk but is now ignored by the build system — a future cleanup bead could remove it, but it is harmless as-is.
B-8d75c57e and B-b7a3bc0c both write to Provider.swift. B-8d75c57e must complete before B-b7a3bc0c since the latter implements timeline(for:in:) which uses WidgetImageCache. The dependency chain is already declared correctly.
Any bead that adds GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE: YES back to AggregatorApp settings will conflict with the new info key. Downstream beads that modify Info.plist keys must use the info.properties section in project.yml, not INFOPLIST_KEY_ build settings.
B-6c345d37, B-59524c20, B-422672f9 all modify WidgetViews.swift. They need to append to the file rather than overwrite it to preserve RadarDefaultBackground. File has a clear MARK comment delineating the section for layout views.
The stub AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry in AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift coexists with the new AggregatorRadarEntry without conflict — both types compile independently. The Provider bead must replace the placeholder.
AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still declares AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry and AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider. The views bead that replaces the stub must remove those types to avoid duplicate-symbol conflicts with WidgetEntry and AggregatorRadarProvider.
None anticipated. ThreadsView is the only file owning NavigationStack path state for the Threads tab. AppRoot tab selection is new state not previously owned by any file.
AggregatorRadarEntry gained two new optional fields (publishedAt, importanceScore). Any downstream bead that constructs AggregatorRadarEntry literals will need to provide these (or rely on the default nil since they are Optional).
WidgetViews.swift defines AggregatorRadarEntry (separate type). Any bead that touches WidgetViews or AggregatorWidgetBundle must reconcile entry types with Provider.swift's WidgetEntry.
Provider.swift has uncommitted changes from a sibling bead in the shared worktree. If that bead commits before this branch merges, there is no conflict with this bead's files. The uncommitted Provider.swift state does not affect ArticleReadStore or ArticleListView.
AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still wires Color.clear as the entry view — downstream beads replacing that placeholder must update to dispatch SmallWidgetView/MediumWidgetView based on WidgetFamily.

last_agent_result.summary: Added 25 unit tests covering all five mandated groups (APIClient URL construction, ImageDownsampler, deep-link parsing, timeline builder contract, ContentSource default). All tests pass; pre-existing 78 tests unaffected.
